incorrects.description: Student set wrong container count on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Count the groups in the example we just looked at.

incorrects.description: Student set wrong container count on second attempt

params.description: Workspace pulses to draw attention to the container count control.

steps.text: Let's think about this together. We're building 4 groups of 3. Start with the groups. How many groups do we need?

incorrects.description: Student set wrong container count three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System sets container count to 4. Four empty containers appear.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. We need 4 groups of 3. First, I set the groups to 4. That gives us 4 containers. The answer is 4 groups.

incorrects.description: Student set wrong items per container on first attempt

steps.text: Check the number in each group from before.

incorrects.description: Student set wrong items per container on second attempt

steps.text: Here's what helps: we're building 4 groups of 3. You set the groups already. Now set how many in each group.

incorrects.description: Student set wrong items per container three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System sets items per container to 3. Three items appear in each of the 4 containers.

steps.text: Let me walk you through this. We have 4 groups. Now I set how many in each group to 3. That fills each container with 3 items. The answer is 3 in each.
